description: Epic single-trip unlocks along the full Eastern Suburbs coastal spine — from the northern tip at Watsons Bay to the southern tip at La Perouse. Day and night variants; complete both for the ultimate badge.

unlock_rules.notes: Trip must not be ended between endpoints. Brief stops (fuel, toilet) OK if trip stays active.

day.label: Daylight
day.rule: Both endpoint visits must occur between official sunrise and sunset (local NSW).

night.label: Night
night.rule: Both endpoint visits must occur between sunset and sunrise — same definition as NSW logbook night hours.

watsons_bay.name: Watsons Bay — harbour tip
watsons_bay.description: Northern coastal extreme: Military Road terminus at Watsons Bay Wharf — the furthest drivable harbour-head point in the play area.
watsons_bay.supervisor_notes: Use the wharf / Military Road end, not a U-turn short of the tip. Ferry wharf and Robertson Park area count as arrival.

la_perouse.name: La Perouse — Botany Bay tip
la_perouse.description: Southern coastal extreme: end of Anzac Parade at La Perouse Point, near Bare Island and the Botany Bay heads.
la_perouse.supervisor_notes: Arrive at the La Perouse village loop / point carpark area — the furthest public driving point on the play-area coast.

expeditions.name: Watsons Bay → La Perouse (Day)
expeditions.description: One continuous daylight drive touching both coastal extremes. Classic Eastern Suburbs learner expedition.
expeditions.supervisor_notes: Plan a clear route via New South Head Road and the coastal corridor. Avoid peak school traffic for the first attempt. All crossed zones must be unlocked for fog tracking.
expeditions.suggested_route_hint: Watsons Bay → Rose Bay → Double Bay → Bondi → Coogee → Maroubra → La Perouse (or reverse).

expeditions.name: Watsons Bay → La Perouse (Night)
expeditions.description: The same coast-to-coast run between sunset and sunrise — counts toward night-driving confidence and logbook Goal 17 practice.
expeditions.supervisor_notes: Only after the day expedition is comfortable. Supervisor stays alert; learner must not touch the phone. Headlights and speed discipline essential.
expeditions.suggested_route_hint: Same corridor as the day run; start after sunset with a full tank and rested supervisor.

validation_logic.summary: Start or resume a trip → enter endpoint A geofence → remain in one trip → enter endpoint B geofence → supervisor confirms.

failure_reasons.trip_ended_early: Trip ended before reaching the second endpoint.
failure_reasons.too_fast: Endpoints reached too quickly — likely not a real coast drive.
failure_reasons.wrong_time_of_day: One or both endpoints outside the required day/night window.
failure_reasons.locked_zone_only: Route valid but fog tracking skipped in unpurchased zones.

_meta.coordinate_note: Endpoint boxes are approximate for v1. Verify at Watsons Bay Wharf and La Perouse Point carpark.
_meta.watsons_bay_note: South Head / Hornby Lighthouse is nearby but mostly walk-in; the wharf terminus is the standard drivable extreme.
_meta.la_perouse_note: Cape Banks tracks further south are largely walking tracks — Anzac Parade end is the drivable extreme.
_meta.monetisation_note: Expedition can complete as an achievement even if some zones are locked; map fog only clears in owned regions.
